summary: Security scan completed. See the findings table below for details.

findings.title: SQL Injection via string interpolation in get_user_unsafe
findings.description: Unsanitized user input is directly interpolated into a SQL query string, allowing arbitrary SQL execution.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to safely include user input.

findings.title: SQL Injection in search_products due to unsanitized f-string query
findings.description: User-controlled input is embedded directly into an SQL statement without sanitization, enabling UNION-based injection.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries to safely handle the user-supplied keyword.

findings.title: SQL Injection in delete_record due to string concatenation with user input
findings.description: The record_id parameter is concatenated directly into an SQL statement without validation or parameterization, allowing injection of additional SQL commands.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries and validate record_id to be of expected type.

findings.title: Command Injection via subprocess.run with shell=True in ping_host
findings.description: User input is passed directly to subprocess.run with shell=True, enabling arbitrary command execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid shell=True. Pass the command and arguments as a list to subprocess.run and sanitize user input.

findings.title: Command Injection via os.system in run_report with unsanitized input
findings.description: User input is directly inserted in an os.system call without sanitization, allowing command injection.
findings.recommendation: Avoid os.system with unsanitized user input. Use safer execution methods and sanitize/validate report_name.

findings.title: Command Injection and Path Traversal via os.popen with user-controlled directory
findings.description: User input is injected directly into a shell command without sanitization, enabling command injection and path traversal.
findings.recommendation: Avoid shell commands with unsanitized input. Validate and sanitize directory input or use Python's os/scandir for file listing.

findings.title: Path Traversal vulnerability in read_file due to unsanitized filename
findings.description: The filename parameter is directly used to build a file path without sanitization, enabling reading arbitrary files via '../' sequences.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize and validate filename input to restrict access to intended directory only, or use secure path handling mechanisms.
